2604.07549.title: EMSDialog: Synthetic Multi-person Emergency Medical Service Dialogue Generation from Electronic Patient Care Reports via Multi-LLM Agents
2604.07549.paper_summary: Conversational diagnosis prediction requires models to track evolving evidence in streaming clinical conversations and decide when to commit to a diagnosis. Existing medical dialogue corpora are largely dyadic or lack the multi-party workflow and annotations needed for this setting. We introduce an ePCR-grounded, topic-flow-based multi-agent generation pipeline that iteratively plans, generates, and self-refines dialogues with rule-based factual and topic flow checks. The pipeline yields EMSDialog, a dataset of 4,414 synthetic multi-speaker EMS conversations based on a real-world ePCR dataset, annotated with 43 diagnoses, speaker roles, and turn-level topics. Human and LLM evaluations confirm high quality and realism of EMSDialog using both utterance- and conversation-level metrics. Results show that EMSDialog-augmented training improves accuracy, timeliness, and stability of EMS conversational diagnosis prediction.
2604.07549.paper_summary_zh: 對話診斷預測需要模型追踪在流式臨床對話中不斷演變的證據，並決定何時確定診斷。現有的醫療對話語料庫大多是雙方對話，或缺乏這種環境所需的多方工作流程和註釋。我們介紹了一個基於ePCR的、以主題流為基礎的多代理生成管道，該管道通過基於規則的事實和主題流檢查，迭代地規劃、生成和自我修正對話。該管道產生了EMSDialog，這是一個基於真實世界ePCR數據集的4,414個合成多發言者EMS對話的數據集，並註釋了43個診斷、發言者角色和輪次級主題。人類和LLM評估確認了EMSDialog在發言和對話級別度量上具有高質量和真實感。結果顯示，增強EMSDialog的訓練提高了EMS對話診斷預測的準確性、及時性和穩定性。
2604.07549.author: Xueren Ge et.al.
2604.07549.authors: Xueren Ge, Sahil Murtaza, Anthony Cortez, Homa Alemzadeh
